description:
You are a platform engineer auditing nightly CI build logs that are stored in plain-text files.  
Your objective is to prepare a very small “build health” report that other tooling in the pipeline will later pick up.

Existing material
1. Two raw log files already exist under  
   /home/user/projects/ci_logs/  
   • logs_2023-11-01.txt  
   • logs_2023-11-02.txt  
   Their exact format is shown below in “Log file format”. DO NOT modify these files.

2. A Git repository is initialised at /home/user/projects/ (there is a .git directory).  
   A file /home/user/projects/.gitignore currently contains only the two lines  
        # Existing ignore patterns  
        *.tmp

Deliverables you must create
A. Directory: /home/user/projects/build_reports/ (create it if missing).  
B. File: /home/user/projects/build_reports/summary.csv – a single, **comma-separated, no-spaces** CSV using the strict layout below.  
C. Files: /home/user/projects/build_reports/failed_builds_2023-11-01.log and  
          /home/user/projects/build_reports/failed_builds_2023-11-02.log – each containing the original log lines whose STATUS is FAILURE for its day, in the order they appeared in the source log.  
D. Update /home/user/projects/.gitignore so that every *.log file inside build_reports will be ignored by Git. Insert the new ignore rule on a fresh line **after** the existing two lines and prepend it with a short comment for clarity (see “Required .gitignore fragment”).

Constraints & hints
• Use awk and/or sed (pipeline is fine) to do all parsing; no python, perl, grep etc.  
• Do not hard-code the counts—derive them from the log files.  
• Keep filenames and directory names **exactly** as spelled here.  
• When editing .gitignore, keep the original two lines exactly as they are; simply append the new comment line followed by the pattern line.  
• After you finish you can run `cat` on the files for your own visual verification, but leave the artefacts on disk – the automated grader will inspect them.

Log file format
Each source log file contains exactly ten lines and every line follows this pattern  
    [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] BUILD_ID=<alphanumeric-or-dash> STATUS=<SUCCESS|FAILURE> DURATION=<integer>s  
There are no blank lines and the final line ends with a newline character (Unix LF).

Required summary.csv layout
1) It **must begin** with this header row (all lowercase):  
       date,total_builds,successful,failed  
2) Subsequent rows: one per log file, ordered by ascending date, each with these four comma-separated fields and **no extra whitespace**.  
Example (these exact numbers will be correct if you parsed the provided sample logs properly):  
       2023-11-01,10,8,2  
       2023-11-02,10,7,3  
The automated test will do an exact string comparison including the newline at the end of every row.

Required .gitignore fragment
Append these two lines **exactly** (note the leading ‘#’ and the indent is only a single TAB or a single space is fine, the grader ignores whitespace here):  
    # build reports – ignore generated log files  
    build_reports/*.log

Verification checklist (what the grader will look for)
• /home/user/projects/build_reports/ exists.  
• summary.csv exists, has three lines, and the lines are byte-for-byte identical to the layout above.  
• failed_builds_2023-11-01.log contains precisely the two FAILURE lines from logs_2023-11-01.txt; likewise the 2023-11-02 file contains exactly the three FAILURE lines from its source.  
• .gitignore is unchanged except for the two additional lines at the end.

Perform whatever terminal actions are necessary to achieve the above state.

/home/user/projects/.gitignore with contents (with newline at end):

Expected final state after successful agent execution
1. Directory /home/user/projects/build_reports/ exists.

2. /home/user/projects/build_reports/summary.csv (newline at end of each line):

2023-11-01,10,8,2
2023-11-02,10,7,3

3. /home/user/projects/build_reports/failed_builds_2023-11-01.log (newline at end of each line):

[2023-11-01 11:12:09] BUILD_ID=build-005 STATUS=FAILURE DURATION=110s
